reasoning: The Lean statement faithfully formalizes the informal Putnam problem. H is the 4D unit hypercube centered at origin ({P | ∀ i, |P i| ≤ 1/2}, side length 1). A "circle of radius r contained in H" is encoded as: there is a 2D affine subspace A (via Module.finrank ℝ A.direction = 2) with a point C ∈ A such that sphere C r ∩ A ⊆ H. Since C ∈ A and A is 2-dimensional, the intersection sphere C r ∩ A = {y ∈ A | dist y C = r} is precisely a circle of radius r in the plane A. IsGreatest contains (√2/2) states that √2/2 is in contains and is an upper bound — correctly capturing "the largest possible radius". The hypercube placement is arbitrary by translation/rotation invariance, so using the centered hypercube is faithful. Degenerate cases (r ≤ 0) don't affect the maximum.
